location.office_time_required: no required commute; optional co-working offices available

level_signals: We're looking for a senior to staff level Product Manager
6+ years experience in the software industry
Leading the development of products end-to-end
Lot of ownership while remaining in the details day to day

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years experience in the software industry
Previous experience in engineering and/or design roles
Shameless curiosity, a propensity to question things deeply
Perform up-front discovery through user interviews, competitive research, data analysis, etc. and present findings to the team with conviction and rigor
This role is open to candidates based in North America or Europe time zones.

scope.team_size_estimate: small focused team (exact size not stated)
scope.surface: Product development system for teams and agents; AI-era software planning, building, and shipping tools
core_responsibilities: Lead the development of products end-to-end, from conception through development and rollout
Perform up-front discovery through user interviews, competitive research, data analysis
Help engineers and designers deeply understand user needs
Craft messaging around upcoming features that resonates with users
Enable sales and marketing teams to effectively communicate with and win with customers

compensation.equity_form: Competitive salary and equity; employee-friendly equity terms including early exercise in the US and extended exercise windows

green_flags: Explicit senior-to-staff seniority signal provides clear leveling
Remote-first with genuine flexibility across North America and Europe time zones
Strong company traction: 25,000+ customers including OpenAI, Coinbase, Ramp
Employee-friendly equity terms (early exercise, extended windows) explicitly called out
Generous benefits: 5 weeks vacation, 4 months parental leave, sabbatical after 4 years
Product-led company with clear ownership model for PM role
red_flags: Visa sponsorship and work authorization not mentioned anywhere in JD
Nice-to-haves section absent — all requirements lumped as hard signals, making it harder to self-assess fit
"World-class design & product taste" is highly subjective with no measurable criteria
Small team and broad ownership expectations may mean high pressure and limited PM peer support

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.manages_people (inferred false; no people management mentioned)
scope.team_size_estimate (inferred 'small focused team' from JD language; not stated explicitly)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from 'product-led company' and customer profile)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred false; single global package implied)
ambiguities: Cities listed (San Francisco, New York, London) are optional co-working offices, not required locations — role is fully remote within North America or Europe time zones.
Work authorization and visa sponsorship are entirely absent from the JD; treated as 'unclear'.
No explicit nice-to-haves section exists; all bullets under 'What we're looking for' are treated as hard requirements per classification rules.
